heading: Effect on rights of States and international commissions
label: 742i Effect on rights of States and international commissions
text: Nothing in this Act shall be construed (1) to interfere in any manner with the rights of any State under the Submerged Lands Act [ et seq.] or otherwise provided by law, or to supersede any regulatory authority over fisheries exercised by the States either individually or under interstate compacts; or (2) to interfere in any manner with the authority exercised by any International Commission established under any treaty or convention to which the United States is a party.43 U.S.C. 1301
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in text, is , , known as the Fish and Wildlife Act of 1956, which is classified generally to sections 742a to 742d and 742e to 742j–2 of this title.
